name: Eekso — The Garden (Ambush)
dmNotes:
SET-PIECE 1 — the flytrap. Structure: LURE → THRESHOLD → CLOSE → FIGHT. The music does the work: start on 'bloom' (genuinely beautiful, zero irony) and hold it longer than feels comfortable. The turn comes from the WORLD first — the plants move, the music follows a beat later.

Threshold = the pool's edge. THE WATER ISN'T REAL — the pool is the thing's STOMACH: clear digestive fluid in a basin of its own flesh. It wraps you up, drags you in, and spines poke you full of holes while the fluid does the slow work. Weak to fire AND slashing (cut the coils). Escape through the sealing archway is a valid win.

STAT BLOCK: 📖 'The Garden of Eekso' (campaign homebrew, 🏠 in the compendium). CR 7 — under moderate budget on paper, the closing-perimeter mechanics make up the rest, and escape stays viable.
scriptText:
# The Garden

You come around the rock and the fortress simply stops being a fortress.

A garden grows here — a real one, walled in old stone, under a hundred feet of black rock. Trees you can't name lean over a pool so clear and blue it looks lit from underneath. Flowers in colours that have no business existing this far from the sun. The air is warm. Birds are singing somewhere in the branches.

After everything behind you, it is the first thing in this place that looks *alive*.

> [!dm] **Play it completely straight.** No ominous adjectives, no knowing pauses. Let them enjoy it; let somebody suggest resting here. The longer the honeymoon, the better the turn.

## The Wrongnesses

> [!dm] Only if a player actively looks — never volunteer:
>
> - **Perception DC 15:** the birdsong doesn't move. Same calls, same places, on a loop.
> - **Nature DC 15:** nothing here has ever *seeded* — no saplings, no dead leaves, no litter. This garden has never been young.
> - **Investigation DC 17:** bones at the base of the trees, half-swallowed by root and moss. Not cleaned up — *grown over*.
> - **Survival DC 13:** no insect noise. In a garden. None.
> - **Nature DC 14 (at the pool):** no inlet, no outlet, nothing living in it — and touching it, the 'water' is faintly warm, slick, and *clings* for half a second before it lets go.
>
> If they clock it early and back out — **let them.** Outsmarting the flytrap is a win. Don't force the fight.

## The Threshold

{{music:wrong}} {{amb:fap-cave-creepy|vol=30%|in=5s}}

- **Trigger:** drinking, wading, or stepping past the pool's far edge

- The birdsong stops — all of it, at once, mid-phrase

- In the silence, every branch in the garden turns

- If they're being *too* careful: a low branch bends down and **offers fruit** to whoever's closest

## The Close

{{music:close}}

The trees are moving. Not swaying — *walking*, dragging their roots out of the soil in long wet handfuls, closing the gaps between their trunks. The flowers turn to face you the way a crowd turns. Behind you, where the archway was, three trunks have already leaned together, and the vines are lacing them shut like fingers.

And the whole garden takes one slow, wet, enormous breath.

> [!dm] Run a **closing perimeter**, not a standing monster:
>
> - Each round the wall of growth advances — the open floor shrinks
> - Ground inside becomes difficult terrain as roots surface
> - End your turn touching the wall → grabbed: **restrained, and pulled**
> - The real question: **can they reach the archway before it seals?** Escape is a legitimate win; killing it is optional.
>
> 📖 **Stat block: 'The Garden of Eekso'** — campaign homebrew, 🏠 in the compendium.

## Fight

{{music:fight}}

> [!dm]
> - **Fire works. So do blades.** Vulnerable to fire AND slashing — a big cut severs a grappling bough outright. Reward both, fast and visibly.
> - Grappled creatures get reeled toward the pool. **The pool is the stomach.** The water was never real — it's clear digestive fluid in a basin of the thing's own flesh. Anyone dragged in is held under, and spines rise out of the fluid and *poke* — acid running straight into the wounds. What hits 0 HP in there goes under and doesn't come back up.
> - Badly hurt, it restarts the birdsong — the same loop, sped up, wrong. It's trying the lure a second time.

## After

{{music:after}}

- If they escaped: the garden is a garden again. Still. Warm. The pool perfectly clear, waiting for the next travellers.

- If they killed it: the pool clouds grey, sinks away, and leaves a bare basin of dead flesh. The whole garden sags where it stands — and you can see it now for what it always was: one thing, wearing a garden.

- Either way, the birds do not come back.

> [!dm] **Loot (approved):** it's in the root-mass, not a chest — a few minutes of digging through wet roots and old bones:
> - **+1 Shield** — an old shield boss showing through the moss; pull it and the whole shield comes up intact, the wood grown over instead of rotted
> - **Scroll of Revivify** — a sealed bone scroll case, still in the hand the roots grew around
> - ~60 gp in mixed old coin, buckles, belt metal — decades of travellers' pocket change

ideas.text: The birdsong is a loop — same calls, same positions. Perception catches it before anything moves.
No saplings, no dead leaves, no seeds. The garden has never been young — it was made, not grown.
If the party is too careful, a branch bends down and offers fruit to the nearest PC
Badly wounded, it restarts the birdsong — sped up and wrong. Trying the lure again.
Restrained creatures get dragged toward the pool. The pool is the mouth.

entities.name: The Garden (lure-predator)
entities.note: 📖 homebrew 'The Garden of Eekso' (CR 7). Lure → threshold → encircle → constrict → digest. Vulnerable to fire AND slashing. Escape through the sealing archway is a valid win.

entities.name: The pool
entities.note: Not water — the STOMACH. Clear digestive fluid in a basin of its own flesh. Holds you under, spines poke, acid in the wounds. What hits 0 HP dissolves.

entities.name: Bones and gear in the root-mass
entities.note: Decades of travellers. APPROVED LOOT: +1 Shield (the shield boss), Scroll of Revivify (bone scroll case), ~60 gp old coin.
